topics.label: Kitchen Gossip
topics.recapText: I already told you about the sous-chef and the butler. Scandalous.

greeting.speakerText: Welcome, welcome! Step into my kitchen: the heart of this establishment! I've got pots bubbling, bread rising, and enough spice to make a dragon sneeze. What can I do for you?

return_greeting.speakerText: Back again! Good, good: a person who appreciates fine food always has a seat at my counter. What's on your mind?

menu_greeting.speakerText: Today's menu! Oh, you're in for a treat. I've been slaving over the stove since dawn and everything is magnificent, if I do say so myself.

menu_soup_response.speakerText: The wild mushroom bisque! Foraged this morning from the forest's edge, simmered with cream and a pinch of thyme. It'll warm you from the inside out. I had a knight weep after his first spoonful: actual tears.

menu_roast_response.speakerText: Slow-roasted boar with honey glaze and roasted root vegetables. Twelve hours on the spit, basted every twenty minutes. I barely slept. But that's what separates a cook from a CHEF, my friend.

menu_special_node.speakerText: The chef's special! Excellent taste. It's my signature fire-seared salmon with a blackberry reduction, served on a bed of herbed rice. Normally that runs fifteen gold: worth every copper.

responses.displayText: Fifteen gold? Fine, here you go.
That sounds extraordinary. You must be the finest chef in the region.

menu_compliment_pass.speakerText: The finest?! Well, I... I wouldn't say... Oh, who am I kidding: yes, I absolutely am! You know what, I like you. Ten gold for the special. Consider it the friends-of-the-chef discount.

menu_compliment_fail.speakerText: Ha! Flattery is an art, my friend, and that was a bit undercooked. Nice try though. Fifteen gold, same as everyone else.

menu_paid_full.speakerText: Wonderful! Coming right up. Find yourself a seat and prepare your palate. This meal is going to change your life: I guarantee it.

poison_greeting.speakerText: I need to tell you something. A stranger came to me last night: hooded, spoke in whispers. Offered me a hundred gold to slip something into Lord Ashworth's evening meal. Poison. I haven't slept since. What would you do?

responses.displayText: A hundred gold is a lot of money. I'll help you do it.
Refuse and report it to the guard immediately.

poison_help_response.speakerText: You'd... really? Just like that? No hesitation? Interesting. Very interesting. Let me ask you something first: does anything about this situation strike you as odd?

responses.displayText: No, not really. When do we do it?

poison_insight_pass.speakerText: Ha! Sharp as a filleting knife, you are. Yes, it was a test: Lord Ashworth's own idea, actually. He wanted to know who in the household could be bought. I'm glad you caught on. You've got good instincts.

poison_insight_fail.speakerText: Never mind. Forget I said anything about it being odd. Look, just... don't mention any of this to Lord Ashworth, will you? I think we both got a little carried away.

poison_help_naive.speakerText: Unbelievable. You'd actually do it. I... I don't even know what to say. I was testing you, you fool! Lord Ashworth put me up to it! Stay away from the kitchen: I don't trust anyone who says yes to murder that easily.

poison_refuse_response.speakerText: That's exactly what I needed to hear. I already reported it, of course: I'm a cook, not an assassin! I just needed to know there are still people with a spine in this town. Lord Ashworth will be glad to hear it. Thank you, truly.

recipe_pass.speakerText: You noticed the saffron, didn't you? Most people can't even identify it. You've got a trained palate: or a trained eye, more like. Fine, I'll share the secret. The trick is a double reduction of the stock before you add the cream, and three pinches of ground fireweed. Not two, not four: three. Write it down before I change my mind.

recipe_fail.speakerText: Ha! You think I'd just hand over my signature recipe to anyone who asks? That recipe has been in my family for four generations. Come back when you've developed a proper palate, friend.

gossip_entry.speakerText: You didn't hear this from me, but the sous-chef has been sneaking off to the wine cellar every evening: and not for the wine. The butler meets him down there. I walked in on them once and they jumped apart like two cats caught on the counter. I don't judge, but the secrecy! In MY kitchen! Nothing stays secret in a kitchen.

taste_entry.speakerText: Perfect timing! I've been experimenting with a new recipe and I need an honest opinion. Here, try this: it's a rosemary-infused lamb reduction with caramelized shallots. Well? What do you think?

responses.displayText: It needs a bit more salt.

taste_delicious_response.speakerText: HA! I knew it! I KNEW it was good! They all said I was mad to try rosemary with the lamb reduction, but who's mad now?! Nobody, that's who! Thank you, friend: you've made my entire week!

taste_terrible_response.speakerText: Terrible?! TERRIBLE?! I spent three days perfecting that reduction! Three days! Do you have any idea what goes into caramelizing shallots at the precise... no. No. Fine. Honesty is... appreciated. I suppose.

taste_salt_response.speakerText: Salt? You think... hmm. Let me try. One pinch, just a... by the gods, you're RIGHT. That's what it was missing! The shallots needed that counterbalance. You've got a real palate, friend. I might have to hire you as my official taster.
